rulings.Case Title: SELMA OZER VS. FRANZ ZENZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Court Directions And To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 1, PLAINTIFF SELMA OZER's Motion For Court Directions And To Reopen Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. In considering the factors set forth in C.C.P. Section 2024(b) the court finds that the likelihood of unfair prejudice to defendant Fluor in allowing three depositions to go forward between now and the March 18th trial date is very high and plaintiff has not provided the court with sufficient evidence of diligence in seeking to depose the three witnesses to justify reopening discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto, Agustin Robleto, Alejandro Robleto And Margarita Lopez'S Motion For Reference To Presiding Judge For Determination Of Presiding Judge'S Designation Of Desired Department
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, Line 27, Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto, Agustin Robleto, Alejandro Robleto And Margarita Lopez's Motion For Reference To Presiding Judge For Determination Of Presiding Judge's Designation Of Desired Department Denied. Plaintiff cites no basis for his motion for reference to the Presiding Judge. Local Rule 2.0 is inapposite and does not support the motion. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks to consolidate this civil action with the pending probate matter, the request is denied. Plaintiff has not shown that consolidation is appropriate. To the extent that Plaintiff seeks a ruling deciding whether this department has jurisdiction to hear this case, the request is also denied. Plaintiff has not shown whether this case involves issues which are within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Probate Court or issues that are merely within the concurrent jurisdiction of the Probate Court and this department. Moreover, even if this case does involve issues within the exclusive jurisdiction of the Probate Court that does not mean that this Court lacks subject matter jurisdiction to hear this case. (See Harnedy v. Whitty (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1333, 1344-1345; In re Michael R. (2006) 137 Cal.App.4th 126, 146; Estate of Bowles (2008) 169 Cal.App.4th 684, 695.) If Defendant fails to object to this department's jurisdiction and instead fully participates in these proceedings, Defendant is barred by principles of waiver and estoppel from raising the issue on appeal. (Harnedy v. Whitty, supra, 110 Cal.App.4th at p. 1345; In re Michael R., supra, 137 Cal.App.4th at p. 146.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiffs are required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Clarification Of The Court Order, To Compel Compliance With The Court Order And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 4, CROSS DEFENDANTs THEODORA ORINGHER, P.C. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION & TODD THEODORA's Motion For Clarification Of The Court Order, To Compel Compliance With The Court Order And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED IN PART. Cross-complainant Mohamad Chahine shall, within 15 days of the date of this order, provide a declaration under penalty of perjury, that he has produced: (1) all invoices for which he seeks compensation from the Theodora Oringher firm as an element of damages and (2) all correspondence with opposing parties, discovery requests, and responses to discovery requests from the other actions for which he seeks compensation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION & TODD THEODORA's Motion To cross-Complainant Mohamad Chahine'S Furher Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Defendant Mohamad Chahine shall, within 30 days of entry of this order serve Theodora Oringher with code-compliant responses to Theodora Oringher's Special Interrogatories - Set Two. The request for sanctions is DENIED. A California Corporation And Todd Theodora'S Notice Of Motion To Compel cross-Complainant Mohamad Chanie'S Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set Two. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Theodora Oringher's motion to compel further responses to requests for production Set Two, is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part as follows: Granted with respect to Requests for Production 26 and 27; Granted in part with respect to requests 27, 28, 30, 31, 32, and 33 but limited to production of insurance policies, declaration pages, renewal statements, policy forms, endorsements, and notices only; Denied with respect to Request No. 29 since it requests information not authorized under CCP Section 2017.210, Denied with respect to requests for production 34 through 40; Granted in part with respect to requests for production 41 through 43 but limited to cancelled checks evidencing payment of attorney's fees; Denied with respect to requests 44 through 47, Granted with respect to requests for production 48 through 50, but the invoices shall be redacted as follows: the hours spent and dollar amounts charged should not be redacted, but attorney-client privileged communications can otherwise be redacted; Denied with respect to request for production no. 60. The requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 5, DEFENDANT WAH-BUN LEE's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED. Plaintiff or her counsel shall, within 10 days of entry of this order, execute a written Kaiser authorization form allowing Kaiser to produce Plaintiff's MOCA (Montreal Cognitive Assessment) test and plaintiff's PHQ-9 (Patient Questionnaire). Defendant Wah-Bun Lee's request for sanctions is DENIED. The declaration of Melvin Honowitz stating that his billing rate is $400 per hour and averring that "Defendant's legal counsel has or will spend 21.25 hours . . . " does not provide the court with evidence in sufficient detail to award sanctions since it does not identify which attorney in the Law Offices of Melvin D. Honowitz & Associates prepared the motion, his or her billing rate, and identify the amount of time actually spent in preparing the motion to compel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SUSAN Y F LEE VS. WAH-BUN LEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Tony Lee And Lai-Sau Lee'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, Line 29, Defendants Tony Lee And Lai-Sau Lee's Motion For Summary Judgment The motion for summary judgment is denied. The motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the 6th cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress. That claim appears to be alleging a negligent conspiracy, a theory of recovery that does not exist. The Court denies summary adjudication as to the remaining claims because defendant failed to maintain its burden of production per CCP 437c. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: ALLIED HEATING & AIR CONDITIONING, INC., A VS. GOTHAM BAY CONSTRUCTION CORPORATION, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, March 8, 2013, at 9:00 am, line 6, CROSS COMPLAINANT BRANDO EMPIRE SAN FRANCISCO, LLC & BRANDY HO's Motion For Protective Order. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED BUT HEARING REQUIRED. Third Party East-West Bank shall not produce any tax returns in response to plaintiff Gotham Bay's November 13, 2012 subpoena. Counsel for plaintiff Gotham Bay and for defendants shall meet and confer concerning the form of the proposed protective order located at Exhibit A to Defendants' motion for a protective order prior to the hearing and shall be prepared to discuss any points of disagreement with the court at the hearing. The request for sanctions is DENIED. =(302/JPT)
